rationale: The cited paper is used in the citing article's conceptual development, not just listed in passing. Kolsarici et al. cite Danaher et al. (2008) to support two specific claims: that competitive advertising interference/clutter can reduce advertising effectiveness, and that SOV-equals-SOM competitive parity is a practice in mature markets. These points are then built into the citing paper's budgeting framework, especially the competitive parity component and its performance interpretation.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Danaher et al. (2008) as prior empirical and conceptual grounding for how competitor advertising and clutter matter to budget setting. This foundation helps justify the inclusion and interpretation of competitive parity and competitive-interference-related reasoning in the citing model.
